$comment: Default soft-scoring weights for Score-AzureRegionFit.ps1. Weights must sum to 1.0. Override by passing a custom weights file to -WeightsFile.
profile_description: Balanced profile for a typical enterprise moving new workloads to an alternative region while keeping the source region as their primary hub. Prioritises service coverage (must run our things), latency (hub-and-spoke pattern implies chatty traffic), and capacity health (want a region that will still be open in 6-12 months).

weight_descriptions.coverage: Fraction of the source region's deployed resource types available in the candidate. Score = coverage percent normalised to [0..1].
weight_descriptions.latency_to_source: P50 RTT from candidate to source region. Score = 1 - min(rtt_ms/100, 1). Below 100ms is 'good enough' territory for most workloads.
weight_descriptions.capacity_health: Ordinal encoding of the Capacity Portal status. Open regions score 1.0, short-term-gap regions 0.7, long-term-gap 0.4, all-restricted 0.0. Unknown = 0.5 neutral.
weight_descriptions.compute_price_delta: % cheaper compute for a representative SKU basket derived from the inventory. Score = 0.5 + clamp(-priceDeltaPct/20, -0.5, 0.5). A 10% cheaper region scores 1.0; a 10% more expensive region scores 0.0.
weight_descriptions.az_support: Fraction of the required AZ count actually available in the candidate. If -RequireAZ is not set this defaults to 1.0 (informational only).
weight_descriptions.sku_portability: Fraction of the source region's SKU families present in the candidate region. Independent of the raw provider coverage — asks whether the SPECIFIC families (Dv5, Ev5, etc.) exist here.
weight_descriptions.egress_cost: Delta between in-region peering ($0.01/GB) and cross-region peering to the source ($0.02-$0.08/GB). Score = 1 - (rate - baseline) / (maxRate - baseline) normalised across all candidates.
weight_descriptions.region_maturity: Higher score for established regions. Recommended regions score 1.0, other regions 0.7, new regions 0.4. Best-effort from az account list-locations metadata.

latency_critical.$comment: For user-facing services with tight response-time budgets.

capacity_first.$comment: For workloads that just need to land somewhere in a hurry (peak season, incident response).

$comment_usage: To use an alternative profile, either pass -WeightsProfile <name> to the script, or copy the profile's weights into your own weights file and pass it via -WeightsFile.
